name: Hit Stun
description: A temporary state after being hit where an actor loses or limits control before recovering.
design_notes: Hit stun defines whether attacks feel impactful and whether combos are possible. It must be balanced with recovery, invulnerability, knockback, armor, cancel rules, and network prediction. Too little hit stun makes hits feel fake; too much removes agency.
design_purpose: Make successful hits visibly and mechanically meaningful.
Create windows for combos, interrupts, or defensive recovery.
Communicate damage and impact without relying only on health loss.
player_fantasy: The player sees attacks interrupt enemies and understands when they are vulnerable after being hit.

parameters.name: hit_stun_frames
parameters.description: Frames of limited control after a hit.

parameters.name: hit_stop_frames
parameters.description: Shared or local freeze frames used at impact moment.

parameters.name: stun_decay_per_repeat
parameters.description: Reduction applied to repeated stun in a combo.

parameters.name: armor_stun_reduction_ratio
parameters.description: How much armor or poise reduces stun duration.

parameters.name: recovery_cancel_frame
parameters.description: Earliest frame where dodge, block, or tech can cancel recovery.

edge_cases: Hit stun during jumps, ladders, grapples, cutscenes, or dialogue needs priority and recovery rules.
Multiple rapid hits should stack, refresh, or decay rather than locking actors forever.
Hit stop and hit stun should use separate clocks so visual freeze does not extend gameplay stun unexpectedly.
Bosses may need partial stun, armor thresholds, or interrupt windows instead of full lockout.
common_bugs: Input lockout remains after stun animation is interrupted.
Hit stop pauses projectiles but not timers, causing mismatched follow-up windows.
Stun applies twice because damage event and animation event both trigger reaction.
Network clients show stagger while server already allowed recovery.
balancing_notes: Long hit stun enables combos but can remove agency or create infinite loops without decay.
Short hit stun preserves control but may make attacks feel weightless unless paired with hit stop or animation reaction.
accessibility_notes: Use clear recovery cues so players know when control returns.
Avoid rapid repeated stun flashes that obscure hazards or cause visual discomfort.

implementation_notes.unity: Model combat timing and hit resolution as data-owned state, drive animation from that state, and apply damage through one authoritative resolver rather than animation-only callbacks.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep combat windows on the actor state, evaluate Area2D/Area3D or shape queries during physics ticks, and emit presentation signals after authoritative damage resolution.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Gameplay Ability System, Actor Components, or replicated combat state for timing and damage authority, with montage notifies only marking windows or presentation events.
implementation_notes.web: Resolve combat in the fixed simulation step from actor state, hit volumes, and attack metadata, then queue UI/audio/camera events from the confirmed result.

example_games.title: Street Fighter II
example_games.note: Uses hit stun and block stun to define attack advantage and combos.

example_games.title: Devil May Cry 5
example_games.note: Uses hit reactions to support juggling and combo flow.
